description: The Bowe Brothers were a criminal family in New York City during the early-to-mid-19th century, headed by Martin Bowe. The gang operated along the New York waterfront, engaging in theft, robbery, and fencing stolen goods. They were known as shooters, cutters, and thieves in the Fourth Ward, leading raids on dockyards and ships. The gang was active from around 1840 to 1860.
